criteria.description: Sean's stylus stays in his right hand in every frame. The right hand grips it near the barrel with three fingers visible — thumb, index, and middle; the left hand never holds the stylus. Visible in front, three-quarter, and profile views; the back view shows only the right arm's silhouette indicating the grip. The stylus is a slim cylindrical drawing pen, roughly one head-height long, tip pointing down-forward in the neutral hold.

Sean's hair rises into a pronounced forward-curling quiff (cowlick) at the front hairline — a single curl that lifts up and flicks forward, and is the highest point of the head silhouette. Visible in front, three-quarter, and profile views; from a top-down or back angle it reads as a small chevron at the crown. The quiff sits at the front center of the head, not the back of the skull.

Sean's hair is dirty-blonde (warm light-brown, palette hair-fill #AD9468 — not platinum, not dark brown), full and textured on top, short and tapered at the sides and back. The top volume is swept up and back from the forehead; the sides are short enough that the ear stays unobscured in profile.

Sean's eyes are blue, set roughly one eye-width apart, almond-shaped, sitting on the horizontal midline of the head. The iris reads clearly blue against the paper-base sclera in colored plates; in line-only motion plates the eye is a simple lidded almond with no iris fill.

Sean wears short, even stubble / a light beard along the jawline, chin, and upper lip, connecting to the sideburns. It is a uniform graphite-hatched shadow following the jaw — not a full bushy beard and not clean-shaven. Visible in front, three-quarter, and profile; rendered as fine cross-hatching in the pencil-test register, never a flat fill.

Sean reads as heroic-realistic adult proportion: head height fits seven times into total body height (1:7), measured crown to sole at neutral stance. This is not chibi and not stylized-tall — it is a working-illustrator's grown-adult build.

At neutral pose Sean's shoulders span roughly 1.5 head-widths; hips are narrower than the shoulders (shoulder-to-hip about 1.4:1), and hip-to-floor is approximately four head-heights. The torso tapers slightly from shoulder to waist — an average adult-male build, neither heavily muscled nor slight.

Sean's default costume is a dark navy short-sleeve crew-neck tee (palette navy-tee #243044) over cool-gray jeans (palette cool-gray-jeans #5C6471). The tee sleeves end mid-bicep; the crew collar sits at the base of the neck. No graphics or logos on the tee.

Sean's jeans are cuffed once at the ankle, ending above gray low-top canvas sneakers (warm-gray, palette sneaker-gray #7E807B) with white soles and laces. The cuff and sneaker are consistent across all full-body views, including the back view, where the jeans show two back pockets.

Sean's colored palette is limited and desaturated: warm cream paper (#F2E6CC) as both background and skin base, warm graphite line (#3D3530), navy tee (#243044), cool-gray jeans (#5C6471), dirty-blonde hair (#AD9468), warm-gray sneakers (#7E807B). The register carries no dedicated skin color — skin is paper-base with cross-hatched shadow. Colors are flat fills with hatched shadows, never gradient-shaded or cel-shaded.

Sean is drawn in the pencil-test-colored register: visible construction lines beneath the final contour, cross-hatching in shadow areas (under the chin, inside clothing folds, under the collar and arms), and a warm cream paper grain reading through the whole plate. The look is traditional pencil animation — not digital vector cleanup, not cel shading, not anime lineart.

Sean's contour line is warm graphite (#3D3530), never pure black (#000000) and never cool gray. Line weight varies HB-through-2B — roughly 1px on interior detail up to 3px on the outer silhouette and weight-bearing contours. Uniform vector-weight linework is a register violation.

Sean's walk cycle is authored for a neutral forward gait at 12fps on twos, derived from the source line-art strip: standard bipedal contact / down / passing / up poses, arms swinging in opposition to the legs (offset, never twinned), the stylus held in the right fist throughout. The Bible covers the neutral gait only — turns, runs, and stops are not authored.

Sean's head turn is a nine-frame sequence. Frame 1 and frame 9 are anchor-grade three-quarter extremes; frame 5 is the looking-down / top-of-crown extreme. The forward quiff stays the highest silhouette point through the turn, and the ear transitions from one side to the other across the sequence. Middle frames 2-4 and 6-8 are interpretive timing in-betweens, line-art only — the rule governs timing and silhouette, not colored-register palette or hatching.
